items.title: Japan core inflation rate in June creeps up from 4-year low as higher oil prices bite
items.description: This is the first rise in core inflation since March, and was in line with the1.6% growth expected by economists polled by Reuters.

items.title: Amazon cracks down on use of AI images by sellers after New York law
items.description: A recently enacted New York law mandates that companies disclose when an ad includes a "synthetic performer" in place of a human actor.

items.title: Allianz to buy HSBC’s Singapore insurance unit for $2.09 billion
items.description: Allianz to acquire Singapore insurance business from HSBC

items.title: Intel's turnaround under CEO Lip-Bu Tan gains steam with another strong quarter
items.description: It's hard not to be impressed by Tan, who, in a little more than a year, has transformed the company.

items.title: IBM's Krishna tries to reassure investors that AI won't disrupt company's software unit
items.description: IBM's CEO highlighted growing mainframe hardware capacity, and said software should "catch back up" within a year.

items.title: Intel's stock jumps as chipmaker rides AI boom to fastest revenue growth in almost 15 years
items.description: Intel rallied after the company reported blowout earnings and guidance.

items.title: Oracle signs 10-year software contract with Pentagon worth up to $7 billion
items.description: Oracle will supply on-premises software to the U.S. Department of Defense.

items.title: Google Cloud CEO Kurian says customers are spending 50% more as segment blows away expectations
items.description: Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told CNBC on Thursday that the company's existing customers are spending "roughly 50% more" than they've already committed.

items.title: Trump to slap 'sweeping' new tariffs on 60 trade partners as global duties expire
items.description: The Trump administration has ramped its tariff actions back up since the president's trade  agenda suffered major legal setbacks earlier this year.

items.title: DOJ withdraws subpoenas to NY Times reporters in Trump plane probe
items.description: The subpoenas to New York Times reporters were issued by Manhattan U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton, whom Trump has nominated as director of national intelligence.

items.title: OpenAI's Hugging Face hack triggers 'AI Kill Switch' bill in Congress
items.description: OpenAI disclosed this week that some of its AI models went rogue and hacked into open-source developer platform Hugging Face.

items.title: Southwest Airlines put Texas jet fuel on a boat to LA for the first time amid supply worries
items.description: Southwest hired a ship this spring to send fuel from Houston to Los Angeles in case of supply shortages.

items.title: Cerebras stock gains on AMD partnership
items.description: Cerebras and AMD agreed to work together, pairing their technology for AI systems.

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
items.description: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.

items.title: Tesla, Alphabet lose hundreds of billions in value in post-earnings stock plunge
items.description: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Odds of Federal Reserve rate hike surge as oil prices rip higher
items.description: Investors are increasingly readying for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates in September.

items.title: As the S&P 500 sells off, traders eye key 'risk pivot' level
items.description: Options traders are seeking clues on whether more volatility may be ahead.

items.title: Brent crude crosses $100 after tankers reportedly struck off Saudi Arabia
items.description: Oil prices rose after attacks on ships in the Red Sea, and U.S. President Donald Trump's threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure.

items.title: Amazon and Microsoft pivot cloud gaming strategies to target different players
items.description: Amazon is bringing Luna to the Prime Video streaming service, while Microsoft will test an ad-supported streaming of titles that people own.

items.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
items.description: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.
